stem.rawText:
9    В”ВӢВғВҗВүВҺВҮВ•вҖ«ВғВҮВ”ВғВҮВҸВғВ•ВҮВҠВ–ВҮВҳВғВҠЬјЬ»ЬәВҶВҗВғЬҘЬӨЬЈвҖ¬ЗӨ
      
      ВҠВӢ ВҠВ‘ВҲВ–ВҠВҮВ•ВҮВҮВҡВ–В”Вғ В‘ВҗВҶВӢВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗВ•ЗЎВ–ВғВҚВҮВҗВӢВҗВҶВҮВ’ВҮВҗВҶВҮВҗВ–ВҺВӣЗЎВҷВ‘В—ВҺВҶВӢВҸВ’ВҺВӣВ–ВҠВғВ–В–ВҠВҮВӣВғВ”ВҮ
        В‘ВҗВүВ”В—ВҮВҗВ–З«

              бҲә1бҲ» вҖ« ЬӨЬЈвҖ¬аөҢ ЬәЬ»вҖ« ЬҘЬӨЬҢЬ–ЬүвҖ¬аөҢ Ь»Ьј

              бҲә2бҲ» вҖ« ЬӨЬЈвҖ¬аөҢ ЬәЬ»вҖ« ЬҘЬӨЬЈЧЎЬҢЬ–ЬүвҖ¬аөҢ вҖ«ЬјЬ»ЬәЧЎвҖ¬

              бҲә3бҲ» вҖ« ЬҘЬӨЬЈЧЎвҖ¬аөҢ вҖ« ЬЈЬҘЬӨЧЎЬҢЬ–ЬүЬјЬ»ЬәЧЎвҖ¬аөҢ вҖ«ЬәЬјЬ»ЧЎвҖ¬


                     CВ‘ВҗВҶВӢВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗбҲә1бҲ»              CВ‘ВҗВҶВӢВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗбҲә2бҲ»              CВ‘ВҗВҶВӢВ–ВӢВ‘ВҗбҲә3бҲ»

solution.rawText:
We work through the conditions in the order given. We recall three relevant conditions for these
two triangles to be congruent:

вҖў    side-side-side (SSS): all three corresponding pairs of sides are equal in length

вҖў    side-angle-side (SAS): two corresponding pairs of sides are equal in length, and the corre-
     sponding included angles are equal

вҖў    angle-side-angle (ASA): two corresponding pairs of angles are equal, and the corresponding
     sides between these angles are equal in length

(1) If we knew that either AC = XZ or the angle B equals the angle Y , then they would
    certainly be congruent by SSS or SAS. We know that the areas are equal, so we have
    2 AB.BC sin B = 2 XY.Y Z sin Y , and so sin B = sin Y . This does not imply that B = Y
    1                 1

    though: we could have Y = 180в—Ұ вҲ’ B, for example:

                             A                                    X




                                             B=Y                      C=Z


(2) We now have an angle at the end of the given side, so we can use the equal-area property
    to work out a second side: the angle вҲ ABC is included between AB and BC, and likewise
    for XY Z. Therefore, as the areas are 12 AB.BC sin(вҲ ABC) and 12 XY.Y Z sin(вҲ XY Z), it
    follows that BC = Y Z. The triangles are therefore congruent by SAS.

(3) We now have two pairs of corresponding equal angles, and so the third pair of corresponding
    angles is also equal and the triangles are similar. Since the areas are equal, they must be
    congruent.

Hence conditions (2) and (3) each imply that the triangles are congruent, and the answer is
option D.
